videos.title: This is Why I Like the PS5: 4 Year Review
videos.transcript:
Hello everyone it's SpawnPoiint and after 
4 years with the PS5 I think it's time for   a long-term review you cannot be grabbing that 
favorite controller sitting back on the sofa and   smashing out those awesome games and over the last 
four years we've had some great new games as well   as new accessories consoles and updates I think 
has made the PS5 even better plus the Jeweler   sense is still my all-time favorite controller to 
use so today I'm going to talk about my experience   with the PS5 what I like and dislike and why I 
think every gamer needs one but before we jump   in drop a like if you are a PlayStation fan okay 
so since launch we've actually had three different   PlayStation 5 consoles to choose from we had the 
original PS5 which launched back in 2020 and looks   like this the slim model which we got in 2023 and 
the PS5 Pro which was released at the end of 2024   now the original and the slim models are basically 
the same other than increased storage from 825 GB   to 1 TB any smaller Slimmer design it didn't add 
any boost in performance this means if you're   still looking to buy a PS5 today or wondering 
if you should upgrade from your OG model other   than size storage and the USB ports on the front 
you're going to get the same gaming experience on   both they both support up to 4K 120 HZ and vrr 
and will play all of the same games and I can   say after thousands of hours of playing on these 
two they are brilliant but if you feel the PS5   still doesn't offer enough power power or storage 
there's always the PS5 Pro the more expensive and   the more powerful PS5 it has a similar design to 
the slim model but with extra vents on the side   and is now the same height as the original PS5 
it also gives us a step up in performance with   an upgraded GPU making a 67% more powerful PS5 
we'll talk about games in a minute but the PS5   pro has been able to take games that already 
look great on the original PS5 and made them   even better so the pro introduced p SSR or the 
PlayStation spectral super resolution this is   an AI upscaler that takes a lower resolution of 
a game say 1440p and upscales it to 4K that then   means you can enable the 120 HZ mode and basically 
Run the game at an upscaled 4K resolution and 120   HZ at the same time now this only works on the 
PS5 Pro enhanced games but we've already had loads   of these added to the library in the first couple 
of months now whether you need a PS5 Pro over the   other two ps5s comes down to if you've got a TV or 
monitor that supports it if you don't have a 4K or   120 HZ compatible screen it's really not worth 
buying one as you're not going to notice much of   a difference now personally for me I have swapped 
to the PS5 Pro as I think the extra storage power   and improv image quality is worth it but it's 
not cheap and you do need to buy a disc drive   separately as it does not come with one although 
if you are upgrading from a PS5 slim that drive   does fit on it but honestly for most people the 
other PS5 are more than enough and don't forget   there is another cool feature with the ps5s and 
it's the fact that you can swap out the covers   so if white isn't your thing you can buy and swap 
out the covers to pretty much any other color now   both the original and the slim models have loads 
to choose from but we are still waiting for the   pro covers to release right okay then we have the 
games after all that should probably be the number   one reason you're buying or even using a PS5 and 
what I can say is over the last four years we've   had some great games to play some exclusive to 
PlayStation and some not but either way I've had   an awesome time so far with it and it's the place 
I spend most of my time okay let me start with   the exclusive games Spider-Man remastered which 
although started off on the PS4 was remastered   for the PS5 and it's also been PS5 Pro enhanced 
so we're getting better visuals and raid tracing   which we never got on the PS4 version and this is 
still one of my alltime favorite games God of War   Ragnarok is also one of the most enjoyable and 
Visually stunning games that you can play on the   PS5 I mean that relationship between kattos 
and atraeus the scenery the fights there's   something special about this game that everybody 
needs to play of course we've got Spider-Man   2 which is a PS5 exclusive game where you get 
to play as both Peter Parker and Miles Morales   I'll talk more about the Dual desense controller 
features in a minute but once you've experienced   the haptic feedback and the Adaptive triggers in 
games like Spider-Man and Horizon forbidden West   you cannot go back to a normal controller these 
make every game so much more engaging then yet   as mentioned we've got the Horizon games so we 
had forbidden West and then more recently the   remaster for zero Dawn both incredible games and 
then some other PlayStation exclusive games have   included Stella blade again one of the most 
vibrant and graphically stunning games that   we've had in this generation rat and Clank Rift 
apart this is one I always go back to Astro bot   is probably one of the best games on the PS5 right 
now and I've spent way too long trying to improve   my speedrun times so much so that I've hit over 
60 hours on this game then we've got some other   Wicked Games as well including the last of us they 
are up there with my favorite stories of all time   I actually think I've played these about three 
or four times each due to the remasters oh and   if you're into Racing We of course have gt7 this 
is the best racing Sim game to play the visuals   the car sounds the gameplay I've played games 
like Forza Motorsport but this one still tops   it in my opinion honestly almost all of these 
games alone should be enough to convince anyone   to get a PS5 although they do eventually end 
up on PC it usually takes a year or two before   they do of course we do get non-exclusive games as 
well games you can find on Xbox and PC to things   like the crew motorfest Hogwarts Legacy blacksmith 
Wukong and Allen weight 2 all of these games are   not exclusive to the PS5 but they still look and 
play great on here as well and then we have the   Xbox games now I do own an Xbox series X and an S 
console and I use them to play the XBox exclusive   games but but recently with the news that more 
and more Xbox games are coming to the PS5 it   feels like the PS5 will end up being able to play 
everything just like on PC so the big one for me   is Forza Horizon 5 this is one that got announced 
within the last couple of weeks and this is one   of the best open world racing games out there and 
the fact it's coming to the PS5 this year is huge   news the same goes for games like Doom of the Dark 
Ages Indiana Jones and more recently sea thieves   we're only a few games away now from having the 
full Library of Xbox games as well I can imagine   gears and Halo being announced in 2025 so does 
that mean this is an Xbox as for the Dual desense   controller is it really that good well even 4 
years on this thing still blows me away honestly   this controller is an absolute GameChanger and it 
really is a console seller when we first got this   I remember thinking how big it felt compared to 
the PS4 controller but today it feels perfect to   hold plus it looks awesome but it's the features 
that this thing has that completely changes the   way the games feel and play firstly the haptic 
feedback which you might think is just another   Rumble motor well it gives you feedback in games 
that you didn't think you could ever feel so in   racing games where you go off the track very 
slightly or break you can feel the controller   vibrate almost like it's simulating the texture of 
the actual Road surface then there's the Adaptive   triggers which again changes the way that all PS5 
games feel so instead of just a trigger that you   pull and it moves you get this resistance as you 
pull it depending on what you're trying to do like   accelerating in the crew motor Fest you can feel 
the trigger almost let off each time it changes   gear or if your will spinning you can feel that 
through the trigger same with breaking the trigger   is firmer to press as you break hard I think at 
first you might think this is more of a novelty   thing to have but having used the Dual desense 
for the last 4 years I don't actually enjoy using   other controllers now on top of that there are 
so many designs out there to choose from that   if you don't like the white one you can go for 
something completely different for the setup in   fact I've lost count how many they've released now 
but we've got so many different colors and limited   edition launches that you can find one for almost 
anything you want over the years I've actually   tried to collect them all although I think the 
only one I'm missing now is Concord as that one   is pretty much impossible to get but don't forget 
we also got the edge controller which is kind of   the Pro controller for the PS5 I use this for the 
games where I either need those extra rear buttons   for the extra button presses or where I want 
the faster trigger response due to the instant   triggers either way this is a great controller 
to use if you want to get the most out of the   PS5 but but let's be honest these controllers 
are not perfect and the only real downside that   I can think of is the battery size typically I get 
around 8 to 10 hours out of them which for most of   us is more than enough for a single gaming session 
but it does mean that you need to remember to   charge it after every single gaming session or is 
probably going to run out the next time and it's   also one of the reasons that I use charging docks 
in my setups as it means I don't have to think or   worry about keeping them charged up for next time 
another thing worth talking about is the portal I   know this isn't directly linked to whether the PS 
PS5 is worth buying but it might make it easier   so the PlayStation portal is a handheld streaming 
device that lets you play your PS5 and your PS5   games away from your setup so it actually does two 
things it can remote play your PS5 so you're able   to log in and play your PS5 on the go so from 
here you're able to see the dashboard and play   your installed library of games obviously this 
locks your PS5 from being used at the same time   as essentially you are just screen sharing but it 
also lets you cloud game directly on the portal   allowing you to play game games in the ps+ Library 
without the need of your PS5 you do need a premium   subscription for this to work but it means you 
technically don't need a PS5 to play these games   now my experience with the portal has been a 
little bit Hit or Miss as sometimes it works   flawlessly thousands of miles away from home and 
other times it fails to log into the PS5 while I'm   sitting in the same room but when it does work and 
my connection is strong enough this is the best   way to play the PS5 on the Move sure I could use 
an iPad and a controller but this is an all-in-one   setup that is definitely the better way to play I 
also wanted to talk about you guys the community   around the PS5 and well PlayStation and gaming 
in general I've been posting content about the   PS5 for years and generally speaking the comments 
replies and Vibe I get across all of the socials   is positive I think that's another huge advantage 
of playing on Playstation is that you're sharing   your love for gaming with hopefully like-minded 
people like we're all using a similar console   with the same controllers and playing the same 
games we all want the best from our game and   experience whether it's new games new updates 
to the UI or just free themes I find that you   guys generally engage with each other a lot 
in the comments too which is great to see so   PlayStation Plus is something that pretty much 
every PS5 owner will use at some point in their   lives it gives us access to online gaming and 
cloud storage but it also gives us free games   every month okay they aren't free as such as we 
are obviously paying a monthly sub but we get   three games every month to download some months 
these games are decent like febrary for example   we get high on life and pay day three I'm happy 
with that whereas we've had some pretty terrible   months as well now personally I will always use 
ps+ as I like the cloud storage knowing that all   of my game saves are backed up online that there 
means the monthly games to me at least are just a   free bonus but there are other plans as well like 
the extra and a premium which gives you a game   library free trials and a classic game catalog 
but unless you're going to make use of those   game cataloges it's probably not worth paying 
the extra I'm premium but that's only because   I stacked these years ago if I was choosing today 
I would probably just stick with essential and if   you're into VR Gaming the PS5 of course has 
psvr too this not only gives us exclusive VR   games like Horizon call of the mountain but some 
games have been upgraded to be VR compatible as   well take gt7 for example this launched as a 
normal PS5 game but since psvr2 released it's   been updated to allow full VR compatibility and 
this has completely changed the way that you play   some of these games like until until you've tried 
VR you don't realize just how good it is now of   course you don't need a PS5 to play VR as you've 
got things like The Meta Quest and other PC gaming   headsets but it's the fact that you can play VR 
on the PS5 if you want to especially if you're a   Trophy Hunter and that leads me onto trophies so 
over the years I've dipped in and out of trophy   hunting where I've suddenly had a surge that I 
want to get as many platinums as I can and then   I'll stop and just enjoy the story and move on 
to the next game but that is what is great about   the PS5 every game has a list of trophies that 
you can go for some are unmissable as they are   part of the story While others are hidden away 
as actas that you need to go looking for but   the UI for the trophies have massively improved 
over the last four years that I think makes it   even easier and more enjoyable to Trophy hunt so 
we've got a full list of what you've achieved here   a platinum counts here for the games you've 100% 
completed and the level that you're currently at   I also like the fact that you can pin trophies 
for the games that you're currently playing so   you can quickly glance at the trophy list if 
you're currently trying to get one so for me   I'm in the process of finishing off watch doog 
Legion now of course trophy hunting is not for   everyone as sometimes it's nice just to play the 
game and move on but out of interest if you are a   Trophy Hunter how many platinums do you currently 
have now something that's often overlooked when   talking about or looking at the long-term reviews 
is the ease of use and how easy it is to jump on   the PS5 and use it on a daily or weekly basis and 
I have to say it is seriously a joy to boot this   thing up every time it's fast it's quiet and I've 
never had an issue with crashes or updates I know   that I can come home grab my controller and power 
it on and within seconds I'm scrolling through the   dashboard and finding the next game to play now 
over the years we've had various updates to the   UI and on the whole every new update improves 
the menus in the UI that you don't really need   to think about what you're doing everything 
is pretty intuitive so all of your games are   obviously listed here we've got completion status 
trophies who else is playing it from your friends   list and I also think the UI on the whole is 
very clean and sharp and I've got to say the   recent addition of the welcome tab is brilliant 
as well I love being able to see all of this in   one place as well as being able to customize it as 
well now when the PS5 first launched I remember us   all being disappointed in the 825 GB of storage 
so it's obviously great to see that the slim and   the Pro Models have both had a bump up in storage 
but with the new games getting bigger and bigger   the internal SSD just gets eaten up so fast but 
fortunately all three of these PS5 models do have   the option to install an additional SSD internally 
which I've actually ended up doing on both of my   ps5s but it also means now that I'm pretty much 
future proof from all of the games I wish to   download so my advice is if you've got a PS5 or 
you're looking to get one definitely increase   the storage via this way as it's so much easier 
to manage another really cool thing with the PS5   is something called PlayStation Stars I mentioned 
this briefly during my top tips video a couple of   weeks back but this is the rewards program that 
PlayStation offer and you get this for playing   games or completing different challenges and in 
turn they give you Collectibles or coins which   you can use to spend on the actual PlayStation 
store so over the years I've earned about 40,000   points which I've redeemed against games and PSN 
credit I can then use that to buy other games on   the store but as you can see here my current 
balance is just under 6,000 I could spend that   on any of these games or save up a little more for 
the more expensive ones essentially you're getting   free money for completing challenges or playing 
games that you would have done anyway now over   the last 4 years we've had so many requests for 
improvements I think we're getting near the end   now like we wanted 1440p and vrr support well we 
got those we wanted folders and they gave us game   lists I think the only real Improvement that I 
can think of if I knew PlayStation were going to   watch this would be to add themes like we had 
these back on the PS4 and they were awesome I   mean sure we have themes or a wallpaper on the 
welcome tab which we can choose and customize   but I want a theme that will work across the 
entire dashboard now I know why they won't do   this and that's because the game Arch would then 
be replaced with your own image but at least give   us the choice if we want to do that but what 
about you guys if you knew PlayStation were   tuned in now and they were reading your comments 
what changes would you like to see added to the   PS5 changes that you think would actually make 
it better but on the whole though I think the   PS5 is seriously an awesome console I jump between 
all the other choices out there from the Xbox to   switch handheld and PC and I keep finding myself 
back on the PS5 every single time I think it's a   combination of the trophy system the exclusive 
games I prefer to play the awesome controller   and the overall look and feel of the console and 
the UI there's something special about it that   I just like to grab my favorite controller and 
sit back on the sofa but if you're on the fence   and you're not sure whether you need a PS5 I'm 
going to say just go for it even if we do get a   ps6 in the next 2 to 3 years don't let that stop 
you there's already a huge library of games to   catch up on that you'll be busy for years to come 
and if you've already got a PS5 which games are   you currently playing that you would recommend 
everyone else try now drop a p PS5 is awesome   in the comments and I'll give you a thumbs up for 
staying right till the end and if you did enjoy   today's video check out my top PS5 tips video 
next as it covers 15 tips to get the most out   of your PS5 well thanks for watching please like 
subscribe and follow me everywhere until next time

videos.title: POCO X8 Pro Max Gaming Phone Unboxing + Handcam (120Hz & MAX Graphics)
videos.transcript: In 2026, gaming phones are getting more expensive than ever. But the real question is, does it actually deliver where it matters most for gamers? Let's find out. Out of the box, you get a type-C cable and a free case. Unfortunately, no charging brick, but it is compatible for 100 watt fast charging. For the design, it ditches the heavy gaming aesthetic for a sleek fiberglass back and a durable metal frame. Plus, it has SGS certified durability and an IP66, IP68, IP69, and IP69K rating. Instead of a bulky physical fan, Poco Pack a massive cooling system inside to keep temps completely under control. It comes in sleek standard colors like phantom black and prism white. But if you want to go all out, Poco is dropping a crazy exclusive Iron Man edition. Now, let's talk about the display. You get a massive 6.83 in AMOLED screen, a smooth 120 Hz refresh rate, and a sharp 1.5K resolution. This M10 panel also delivers an insane global peak brightness of 3500 nits, ensuring you can game perfectly even in direct sunlight. Plus, it features a lightning fast 3D ultrasonic fingerprint sensor. Let's see if the performance lives up to the design. This phone runs on the Demenity 9500S. As for spec varants, you can get up to 12 GB of RAM and 512 GB of storage. Let's see how the phone does with the heavy hitter games that we love. All right, first up, we have Call of Duty Mobile. I'm just swiping left and right. Actually feels pretty good. Consistent 120fps, which is expected for a phone like Poco and a game like Call of Duty Mobile. And just like that, we have ultra HDR. So, let's equip that. So, it's capable of playing at ultra HDR. Extreme HDR is, you know, it's not available right now. The game allows us to do that even with this phone. So, that means it must be good. So far, the phone's temperature is all right. You know, just just regular heat. It's nothing super hot or anything. It's not like what you would get with your regular daily phone, which is why I'm going to start putting this jelly case. Yo, that was lots of fun, bro. any uh heat from the case. Heat ain't even going through the uh the case, man. Oh my gosh, bro. 23 kills. We went crazy that game.
